-128 J Street r Suite 620 e Sacramento, CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
January 2l,20lO
Craig Swieso
16 Marjoram Court
Sacramento, California 958 l4
RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. .{-10-207
Dear Mr. Swieso:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the post_governmental
employment provisions of the Political Reform Act (the *Act'{r itr'is;r?; i, uor"a on the facts
presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder
of fact when ir renders assistance. (In re oglesby (igls) I Fppc ops. 71.)
Additionally, our advice is limited to obligations arising under the Act. we do not
address the applicability, if any, of other post-govemment employment laws snch as public
Contract Code Section l04l l.
QUESTION
May you attend and participate in a public hearing before your former employer on a
proposed regulatory amendment in which you participateld during your "*ptoy-ent if your
attendance is to represent yourself and you are not compensatea uy another pe'rson?
CONCLUSION
Yes' Because.you are not being compensated for your attendance at the meeting, you
may attend and participate in the hearing forihe purpose of ,.p.rr"nting your own interests.
FACTS
You are curently an attomey with the Franchise Tax Board (FTB) and will be
'state service in January 20ll to take a position with a national accounting firm. over
leaving
the past
I The Political Retbrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g1000 rhrough gl0l+. All srarurory
ref'erences are to the Government Ctlde' unless otherwise indicated. ihe regutations of the Fair political practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8 I l0 through 18997 of ritle 2 of thi Califbrnia Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2' Division 6 of thi Calitbrnia Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No.A- lO-207
Page No. 2
year you have been involved with drafting amendments to california code of Regulations,
title 18, section 25106.5-1. on April zt,.1oroo"d s;;;ber22,2olo,you acted as a hearing
officer during public meetings whirein the proposea o'-lna-"nts were discussed. This process
is still in the informal stage.lhe for.not regutotory p;;;;; with the office of Administrative
Law (oAL) has not.yet b"-gul. The propos"d orn*t*ents wil very likely be the subject of
another public meeting, which w1l occtu after you trou".iut" service.
You wish to known if you can participate in any future public meeting involving the
proposed amendments as an interested party and not as a formai representativ"e of any person.
Because you will only be representing youiself, you will not be compensated by anyone else for
your participation.2 
e J
ANALYSIS
Public officials who leave state service.are subject to two types of post-governmental
employment provisions under the Act, colloquially k";;;;r the .,revolving door,, prohibitions.
In addition' section 87407 prohibits certain siate and locai officials from ,r"oting, participating in
making' or using their official position to influence decisions affecting persons with whom thev
are negotiating employment, or have any iurangement concerning employment.i'rorlo I"t "'"'
Regulation 18747.)
Post-Governmental Employment provisions
The one-Year Ban- The "one-yenr ban" prohibits a former state employee from
appearing before or.communicating wiih,to, coip"n*rlon,his or trer rormer agency for the
purpose of influencing any administrativsor t"gisrutiu" a"iio^ o, ony air"rJonary act involving
the issuance' amendment, awarding' or,revogation of a permit, Iicense, grant, or contract, or the
sale or purchase of goods or prop"iy. (See Section g;ioe:;Reguration rg746.r.)
The one-year ban applies to any employee of a state administrative agency who holds a
position that is designated oi should ue aesignated in trr" og"n"y's conflict-of-interest code.
(Section 87406(dxl); Regulati on 18746.1<iltzl.li irr" i.i applies for tzmonrhs from the dare
the employee Ieaves state office or employmeni,'which is aerinea as the aut. ttL employee
permanently leaves state service or takls o l.or. orausence. (Regulati on lg7i46.l (bXl) and (2).)
2 You have indicared that your "employer will be paying [you your] basic salary.,' we assume that this
;*ffi*'J;;:,fffi:ll':f;t,loJo'n'o'fn io, vo, ott.noon"T Jt ti. a",.,iu"o o.iiuity a,J that you wiu be
I Because you have already lefi state service, we do not address this issue. The commission does not
provide advice with respect to past conduct.
'' A governmental employee should be designated in his or her agency,s conflict-ot-interest code if the
employee makes or participates in making Sovernmental decisions that have a reasonably tbreseeable material etl.ect
on any flnancial inrerest. (Section g:fi}21


File No.A- l0-2O7
Page No. 3
while in effect, the one-year ban applies only when a former employee or official is
being compensated for his or her appearances or communications before his or her former
agency on behalf 9f any person as an agent, attorney, or representative of that person.
(Regulation 18746.1(b)(3) and (a).) 
J '
In contrast to the permanent ban,.which only applies to "judicial or quasi-judicial,,
proceedings' the one-year ban applies to "any opp"or-"" or comrnunication made for the
purpose of influencing administrative or legistaiive action or influencing any action or
proceeding involving the issuance, amendment, awarding, or revocation of a permit, license,
grant' or contract, or the sale or purchase.of goods or property." (Regula tion lg746.l(b)(5).) An
appearance or communication is for the "purpose of influLncing" if iiis made for the ;prir"ipri'
purpose of supporting' promoting, influencing, modifying, opposing, delaying, or advancing the
action or proceeding." (Regulation 187462.1 A, opp"*once or communication includes, but is
not limited to, conversing by telephone or in person, foo"rponding in writing or by electronic
transmission, attending a meeting, and deliveiing orsending any communica tion. (Id.)
S ection 8ZO}Z(a) defines ..admini strative action,, as :
" (a) 'Administrative action' means the proposal, drafting, development,
consideration, amendment, enactment, or difeat by any state agency of any nrle,
regulation, or other action in any ratemaking procieding or *iqu;i-r"glir"ii".j
proceeding, which shafl incrude.lny proceeJing gou"*.a uy itrapter:.s
(commencing with section 113a0) oiParr I ofblvision I oirniz..
Section 82037 defines ,.legislative action,, as:
"'Legislative action' means the drafting, introduction, consideration,
modification, enactment or defeat of any bill, resolution, amendment, report,
nomination or-other matter by the Legisiature or by either house o, uny
cornrnittee';u-bcgmmittee, joint or select committee thereof, or by a #mber or
employee of the Legislature acting in his official capacity. .Legis'lative action,
also means the action of the Governor in approving or vetoing any bill.,,
Finally, appearances and communications are prohibited only if they are before a state
agency that the public official worked for or r"pr.rrnr"Jor a state agency ,.which budget,
personnel, and other operations" are subject to ih" control of u rtut" "ug;rly 
itre puutic official
worked for or represented. (Regurati on_1u+6.1(bx6)., This would appry to any covered
communications you may have with FTB.
However, not all communications are prohibited by the one-year uarl. appearances or
communications before a former state agency employer, made u, portof ,.r"-i."s performed to
administer, implement,.or fulfill the requirements of an existing permit, license, grant, contract,
or sale agreement may be excluded from the [one-yearJ prot iuition, . , . p.riJ..r ,n. services do
not involve the issuance, amendment, awardirg, oi..rl.otion of any of these actions or


File No.A- 10-207
Page No.4
proceedings." (Reguration lg746.l(b)(5)(A); euiringAdvice Letter, No. A-03_2 7zi Hanan
Advice Letter, No. I-00-209.)
Additionally' Regulation t8746.2(bXl)-(4) provides that appearances or commlrnications
are not restricted under the one-year ban, if an inaiviauu: 
-
"(l) Participates as a panelist or formal speaker at a conference or similar public
event for educational purposes or to disseminate research and the subject matter
does not pertain to a specific action o. p.o.."Jing,
"(2) Attends a general informational meeting, seminar, or similar event;
"(3) Requests information conceming any matter of public record; or
"(4) Communicates with the press.,,
we have also advised that a former agency official may, without violating the one-year
ban' draft proposals on a client's behalf to belubmitt"J lo the agency so long as the former
employee is not identified in connection with the clienis efforts to influence,administrative
action' (cook Advice Letter, No. 4-95-3 2l; Harrison Advice r"tt"., No. 
- 
i-gz-zsg.) similarly,
a former agency official may Lrse his or her expertir" to oauir. ctients ;;rh; procedural
requirements, plans, or policies of the offici.rt r".-* ["n"y so long as the employee is not
identified with the employer's efforts to influenc. ,h. d;n" y. (perryAdvice Letter, No. A-94-
004.)
The Permaner.tt Ban- The "permanent ban" prohibits a former state employee from
"switching sides" and particip ating,for compensation, in any specific proceeding involving the
state of california or assisting othiis in the'pr"r"aini-iiir," pio""rJiig-ifie in which the
former state employee participated while "rnpfo1r.JUfri" rro,.. (See Sections g7401_g 7402;
Regulation lS74t.t.)
The permanent ban is a lifetimetan and applies to any judicial, quasi-judicial, or other
proceeding in which.you participated while you r"*ra as a state administrative official.
"'Judicial' quasi-judicial or other proceeding' -"un, ory plceeding, application, request for a
mling or other determination, coniract, clairi, .g{;;i 
investigation, charge, accusation,
arrest or other particular matter involving.e specific party or parties in any court or state
administrative agency ' ' '." (Section az4oo1").1 eaiirionotty, an official is considered to have
'participated" in a proceeding if he or *",l"tn ill;1il;roceeding ..persona1y, and
substantially through decision, approval, disapprovar, ro.rir *ttt"ri.r"orr*artion. rendering
advice on a substantial basis, lnvestigation, oi ir. of conficlential intbrmation . . ..,, (Section
87400(d).)
"The permanent ban does not apply to a 'new' proceeding even in cases where the new
proceeding is related to or grows out oi a irior pro...alng in which the official had participatecl.
A 'new' proceeding not sllbject to the p.rron"nt ban typi"caily involves aitrerent parties, a


File No.A- 10-207
Page No. 5
different subject matter, or different factual issues from those considered in previous
proceedings." (Risr Advice Letter, No. A-04-lg7; also see Donovan Advice Letter, No. I_03_
I l9') New contracts.with the employee's former agency in which the former employee did not
participate are considered new pro""laing s. (tesli iiii"" t"tt"r, No. I-gg-64g.) A new
contract is one that is based on new "ontid"roiion and new terms, even if it involves the same
parties' (Ferber Advice Letter, No. I-99-10 4; Anderso, eoui." Letter, No. A-9g-159.) In
addition' the application, drafting, and awarding of a contract, Iicense, or approval is considered
to be a proceeding separate fromJhe monitorin! orra p".ro*ance of the contract, Iicense, or
approval. (Anderson, supra; BlonienAdvice f-"etter, foL. e_Ag_aOt.l
Because you,are not being compensated to appear and participate in the public meetings
held by the FTB' and you are attJndins t"rtll to.r"piJr"nt your o*n interert, neither the one-year
ban nor the permanent ban apply to yo"u in this ,it,rition-'"'
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By:
J. Lenkeit
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
